resolved.note: Resolved by replacing ambiguous string-splitting calls with explicit character arrays so the Windows-targeted test project compiles cleanly.

Resolved by redacting exact sensitive metadata values from diagnostics free-form text so DiagnosticsLoggerTests and release smoke pass again, and by capturing packaged-smoke exit codes with Start-Process -PassThru -Wait in the Windows package validator.

Resolved by making scripts/build_dmg.sh invoke dmgbuild through the active virtualenv Python module instead of the fragile bin/dmgbuild entrypoint, which carried a stale shebang from another checkout and broke local DMG packaging.

Resolved by making scripts/build_dmg.sh run scripts/release_smoke_test.sh by default, which now includes the startup keychain smoke; local DMG packaging was rerun successfully through that hardened preflight path.

Resolved by running GitHub-hosted CI run 23988211616 and unsigned Release workflow run 23988213079 on commit eca6ba9 after upgrading actions/setup-python to v6; both workflows completed successfully without the prior Node 20 deprecation annotation.

unresolved.note: Real Windows desktop validation is still required for tray, recording, screenshot, and hotkey behavior.

CI now covers phase branches, both Windows test projects, package validation, packaged-app smoke execution, packaged-file hash parity, uploaded validation evidence artifacts, and a Windows Codex handoff artifact, but the WindowsDesktop test host and full interactive runtime behavior still require Windows.
Live VoiceOver and hosted docs-site accessibility validation still remain for a later remediation phase.

phase/bootstrap now patches the docs-site dependency tree locally for serialize-javascript 7.0.5, brace-expansion 1.1.13, and express-path-to-regexp 0.1.13, and the Docusaurus production build passed on the patched graph; the risk remains open until the remediation is pushed through hosted CI and merged to the default branch.

Local npm 11.12.1 on Node 25.9.0 fails registry fetches with EBADF during npm install for the docs site even though curl can reach registry.npmjs.org; OPS-012 used direct tarball extraction as a temporary workaround, but the standard npm install path is still unreliable in this workspace.
